Title: Maize leaf yellowing gene ZmCAAX modulates growth and drought resistance by regulating abscisic acid contents through interaction with the ABA biosynthetic enzyme ZmNCED3

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes drought response including stomatal closure and stress adaptation; carotenoids function in leaf pigmentation and as ABA precursors
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: ZmCAAX negatively regulates ABA accumulation and drought resistance, while promoting lower carotenoid degradation under normal conditions; loss of function causes leaf yellowing with reduced carotenoid/chlorophyll but increases ABA and drought tolerance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Map-based cloning identified ZmCAAX as the causal gene for the maize yellow plant 1 mutant. 2. ZmCAAX encodes a chloroplast-localized CAAX amino-terminal protease family protein highly expressed in leaves. 3. ZmCAAX knockout mutants show yellow leaves, elevated ABA, reduced stomatal aperture and enhanced drought resistance, whereas overexpression causes drought sensitivity. 4. ZmCAAX physically interacts with ZmNCED3 and promotes its degradation, thereby modulating carotenoid degradation and ABA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: CAAX amino-terminal protease family protein; CAAX domain; two transmembrane domains
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in leaves, especially leaves with elevated chlorophyll; downregulated by ABA and 300 mM mannitol/drought treatment
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: ZmUbi promoter (constitutive overexpression); CaMV 35S promoter (transient expression/localization assays)
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; 300 mM mannitol; exogenous ABA; constant light for chloroplast ultrastructure observations
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: map-based cloning; allelism test; EMS mutants; overexpression; transcriptomics (RNA-seq); hormone/metabolite quantification; drought phenotyping; Y2H; Co-IP; BiFC; LCI; cell-free degradation assay; transient coexpression assays
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout improves drought tolerance but impairs chloroplast development, causes leaf yellowing, delays flowering, reduces photosynthetic efficiency and is discussed as likely reducing grain yield; overexpression increases drought sensitivity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Knockout, downregulation, or favorable natural alleles could improve drought resistance and ABA-mediated adaptation; expression fine-tuning using CRISPR/Cas9, RNAi, EMS, haplotype selection, or drought-repressed promoters is proposed to balance drought tolerance, carotenoid traits, and yield
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, NGDC: CRA022233; gene accession in MaizeGDB: Zm00001d025860
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Silencing of a tobacco CAAX homolog (NTAB0984080) enhanced drought tolerance, suggesting conserved negative regulation of drought resistance. ZmCAAX is not established as an enzyme in ABA synthesis; it regulates metabolite levels via protein stability of ZmNCED3.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA mediates drought adaptation, including stomatal closure and enhanced drought resistance
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: carotenoid cleavage step leading to ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-epoxycarotenoids to produce xanthoxin, the committed precursor for ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: committed step; branch point from carotenoid pool to ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies immediate committed precursor for ABA biosynthesis; promotes carotenoid degradation toward ABA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key rate-limiting enzyme in ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher ZmNCED3 protein abundance promotes ABA biosynthesis and drought resistance, while ZmCAAX-mediated degradation lowers ABA and increases carotenoid retention
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ZmNCED3 physically interacts specifically with ZmCAAX in chloroplasts. 2. ZmNCED3 transcript abundance is unchanged, but its protein abundance rises in zmcaax mutants and falls in ZmCAAX overexpression lines, showing post-translational regulation. 3. Coexpression with ZmCAAX lowers ABA and raises carotenoid levels, indicating that ZmNCED3 links carotenoid cleavage to ABA production. 4. The paper supports ZmNCED3 as a central positive determinant of drought-responsive ABA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Protein accumulates more when ZmCAAX is reduced and under drought-associated conditions inferred from decreased ZmCAAX; transcript unchanged across genotypes tested
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter in transient coexpression assays
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Drought stress; 300 mM mannitol; cycloheximide; MG132; E64d
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; beta-gal assay; Co-IP; BiFC; LCI; immunoblotting of protein abundance; cell-free degradation assay with CHX/MG132/E64d; transient coexpression followed by ABA/carotenoid quantification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher ABA via ZmNCED3 is linked to stronger drought resistance but likely growth/yield tradeoffs through altered chloroplast function and stress-growth balance
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Stabilizing ZmNCED3 protein or reducing its degradation via ZmCAAX editing could enhance drought tolerance; should be balanced against possible growth and yield penalties from excessive ABA/carotenoid depletion
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Interaction with ZmCAAX is specific; ZmCAAX does not interact with the homolog ZmVP14. Enzymatic activity per se did not differ among genotypes, indicating protein abundance/stability rather than catalytic alteration is the main mechanism.
